title: Selections from Early Middle English, 1130–1250: selected texts from Hall's 1920 edition
summary: CME00035 is a U-M electronic selection from Joseph Hall's 1920 anthology, not one medieval work and not the complete printed volume. It preserves the title page and printed pp. 1–93, 117–148, and 176–222: selections I–XIII and XVI–XIX, only the first page of XX, and selections XXI–XXIII. Hall edited or collated these texts from twenty-three distinct surviving manuscript objects. This record separates repository XML, U-M encoding, Hall's scholarly edition, physical witnesses, and digital reproductions; records exact source roles and work portions; and caches only complete published provider objects where terms and practical retrieval allow. Complete-publication coverage is not conflated with complete physical-object photography, and private research retention is kept separate from redistribution, publication, and commercial permission.

open_questions.question: Can a complete color facsimile of the entire current bound Worcester Cathedral Library MS F.174 be obtained and retained locally?
next_steps: Ask the Cathedral Library whether complete existing photography or microfilm exists.
If commissioning photography, request covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, endleaves, blanks, every leaf, adjacent works, and edge views if offered—not only ff. 63r–64r.
Record ff. 63r–64r separately as the Hall work portion with exact image locators and first/end deep links.
Confirm private retention, redistribution, scholarly publication, and commercial use separately in writing.

open_questions.question: Can complete current-bound-object facsimiles be obtained for the Hall witnesses without verified public complete images, including Royal 5 F VII, Harley Charter III B 49, Vespasian A XXII, Nero A XIV, Titus D XVIII, Cleopatra C VI, Stowe 34, Otho C XIII, Royal 17 A XXVII, and Arundel 292?
next_steps: Ask BL Reference Services whether complete photography, legacy microfilm, or unindexed internal surrogates exist for each shelfmark.
Request each complete physical object or separately cataloged component, including covers, spine, pastedowns, endleaves, blanks, adjacent works, damaged fragments, and edge views if available.
For Harley Charter III B 49, first obtain the exact current catalog identifier and shelfmark crosswalk.
Keep private-retention permission distinct from redistribution, publication, and commercial licensing.

open_questions.question: Can complete reusable facsimiles be verified or obtained for Lambeth Palace MS 487, Caius MS 234/120, Bodleian MS Bodley 34, and Bodleian MS Laud Misc. 471?
next_steps: Resolve Lambeth's current Verify Access barrier and ask whether the two LUNA results cover the whole physical object or only selections.
Ask Caius for a modern catalog crosswalk and complete-object imaging route.
Ask Bodleian for exact catalog identifiers and complete existing or commissioned photography of Bodley 34 and Laud Misc. 471.
Request bindings, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, all leaves and adjacent works; document delivery and publication terms separately.

open_questions.question: What exact folio and line spans from Cotton Nero A XIV, Titus D XVIII, and Cleopatra C VI underlie every base, inserted, and variant reading in Hall selection IX?
next_steps: Collate Hall pp. 61–75 and the separate notes volume against the three witnesses.
Record Nero's exact selected base run, every Cleopatra insertion between asterisks, and Titus/Cleopatra apparatus ranges.
Do not replace these roles with one undifferentiated Ancrene Wisse source relation.

open_questions.question: Why were Hall XIV–XV and most of XX omitted, and what initial keying, source-image, correction, and reuse history precedes the documented 2016 CME review?
next_steps: Request item-level production metadata and any downloadable source package from U-M.
Confirm whether the repeated REF 156 / p. 148 PB at the Saint Juliana–Owl shared-page division was intentional in U-M's production structure.
Keep TEMPHEAD scoped to 2016 validation and proofing rather than initial creation.
Do not infer OCR versus manual keying from the current XML alone.

open_questions.question: What permissions are required for publication, redistribution, or commercial use of the cached noncommercial manuscript bundles and the uncached British Library viewer images?
next_steps: Treat Bodleian, Trinity, and Parker CC BY-NC 4.0 bundles as private/noncommercial research copies unless the proposed reuse is plainly licensed.
Use Jesus College's CC BY 4.0 attribution and permalink requirements for reuse of Jesus 29 images.
Ask Parker and relevant libraries separately about high-resolution scholarly publication and any commercial use.
For British Library images, resolve UK status and manuscript-reuse terms before local bulk caching or publication.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME, CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00035.xml, pinned commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: Repository source artifact; title page, identifiers, textual hierarchy, page breaks, folio milestones, notes, and 2016 TEMPHEAD review statement.

evidence.citation: Local XML and archive inspection for CME00035.
evidence.locator: BODY/DIV1, PB, P, LG, L, MILESTONE, NOTE; comparison with Hall pagination
evidence.summary: Established 21 numbered body units; 174 PB elements but 173 unique PB references, 110 P, 30 LG, 2,717 L, and exact coverage of title page plus pp. 1–93, 117–148, 176–222. REF 156 is repeated for the shared p. 148 boundary between Royal Saint Juliana and Owl.

evidence.citation: Legacy CME image archive CME00035.zip.
evidence.summary: An intact 173-TIFF archive with image runs 00000001, 00000009–00000101, 00000125–00000156, and 00000184–00000230. These match the 173 unique PB references exactly; the 174th PB element repeats REF 156 for shared printed p. 148.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, CME00035.
evidence.summary: Item-level catalog, citation, public text, Hall publication details, reader subject, and No Copyright–United States signal.

evidence.citation: Joseph Hall, ed., Selections from Early Middle English, 1130–1250, Part I: Texts (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1920), complete lithographic-reprint scan from first-edition sheets.
evidence.locator: title page, preface, table of pieces and manuscripts, pp. 1–222
evidence.summary: Complete Part I reproduction establishing all twenty-three pieces, pagination, editorial frame, source table, and omissions in CME00035. The scanned copy is a lithographic reprint from first-edition sheets whose title verso lists reprints through 1958, not the 1920 physical impression.

evidence.citation: Hall 1920, Preface and Table of Pieces and Manuscripts; searchable Project Gutenberg transcription.
evidence.locator: Preface; Table of Pieces and Manuscripts; manuscript-description summaries before each selection
evidence.summary: Documents Hall's selection rationale, tentative chronology, source-following punctuation, intervention signs, manuscript collation, and item-by-item witness roles.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive item p1selectionsfrom00halluoft, metadata and complete Part I PDF derivative.
evidence.summary: Identifies the complete Part I scan and file derivative; direct title-verso inspection shows that the scanned copy is a lithographic reprint from first-edition sheets, with reprint dates through 1958.

evidence.citation: Joseph Hall, Selections from Early Middle English, 1130–1250, Part II: Notes (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1920), complete Internet Archive scan.
evidence.locator: title page; manuscript descriptions and notes pp. 223–675
evidence.summary: Direct PDF inspection establishes that this generically cataloged item is Part II: Notes, not Part I. It supplies Hall's detailed manuscript descriptions, linguistic analysis, introductions, commentary, glossary, and index.

editorial_practices.description: Hall assembled twenty-three separately transmitted pieces as a teaching anthology and called their order tentatively chronological; he explicitly noted that XVII and XIX were misplaced and that VII was moved beside VI because he considered it derivative.
Hall states that the texts follow the manuscripts in detail and retains manuscript punctuation instead of imposing modern punctuation.
editorial_practices.scope: Hall's edited texts, subject to the specific base and parallel witnesses named for each selection

editorial_practices.description: Hall reports collating the manuscripts; in selection IX he identifies Nero as base, Cleopatra passages inserted between asterisks, Titus and Cleopatra variants, and a siglum for their agreement against Nero.
editorial_practices.scope: Varies by selection; exact source roles are recorded in relations

editorial_practices.source_feature: Abbreviations, obvious errors, additions, and interlineations
editorial_practices.representation: Expanded letters in italics; corrections footnoted; additions in square brackets; interlineations between accents
editorial_practices.description: Hall's preface defines visible intervention conventions rather than presenting an unmarked diplomatic transcript.
The electronic text retains the title page and printed pp. 1–93, 117–148, and 176–222 while omitting preliminaries, selections XIV–XV, and pp. 149–175 of selection XX.

scope.notes: Selection XIV is wholly omitted from CME00035; CME00035 also omits the Caligula presentation of XX.

subject.description: Parallel text in selection XIV, Laȝamon

scope.notes: Selection XIV is wholly omitted from CME00035.
Selection XV is wholly omitted from CME00035.

Sawles Warde ending, Saint Katherine, and Royal Saint Juliana presentation
Selection XXII, Joseph and His Brethren

object.description: Five sermons in the second manuscript component

review_notes: The primary cataloging subject is the discontinuous 21-piece electronic selection; Hall's complete 23-piece Part I is its source edition and a distinct entity.

Hall's manuscript table distinguishes direct text, parallel copies, inserted passages, variants, and consulted witnesses. Relations preserve those differences rather than labeling every manuscript an equal base text.

A complete cached IIIF bundle means every canvas in the provider's published manifest at review time. Trinity B.14.52 and the Parker objects omit some exterior views such as spine or edges, so their bundles are complete published inventories but not proven exhaustive photography of the physical objects.
Jesus College MS 29 uses a legacy foliation in Hall that is consistently 73 higher than the current Digital Bodleian labels: 229=156, 252=179, and 262=189. Both systems are recorded.

Internet Archive's generically titled selectionsfromea00halluoft PDF is Part II: Notes on direct title-page inspection. The complete Part I image source used here is p1selectionsfrom00halluoft, a lithographic reprint from first-edition sheets whose title verso lists reprints through 1958; neither layer is mislabeled as a scan of the 1920 Part I physical impression.
No British Library IIIF object was bulk-cached: current BL terms and the user's non-systematic-extraction constraint were treated conservatively even where complete public viewers exist.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00035
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's publication workflow, supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID all identify CME00035.
TEMPHEAD records July 2016 review, validation, proofing, and structural changes; it is not initial-production provenance.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item CME00035
entities.description: A public electronic selection from Hall's 1920 Part I, preserving twenty-one numbered pieces and the title page but omitting two complete pieces and most of a third.

entities.label: Joseph Hall, Selections from Early Middle English, 1130–1250, Part I: Texts (1920)
entities.description: Hall's complete anthology of twenty-three numbered selections with a preface, manuscript table, source-following texts, corrections, and collated variants. Hall presents the arrangement as tentatively chronological and explicitly describes manuscript punctuation and intervention signs.

bibliographic.citation: Joseph Hall, ed., Selections from Early Middle English, 1130–1250, Part I: Texts (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1920), vii, 222 pp.
bibliographic.title: Selections from Early Middle English, 1130–1250. Part I: Texts

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of a lithographic reprint of Hall's 1920 Part I

notes: The copy states that it was reprinted lithographically from sheets of the first edition and lists reprints through 1958; it retains the complete Part I and surrounding scan matter.

entities.label: Project Gutenberg eBook 26413
entities.description: A complete searchable transcription of Hall's Part I with transcriber-supplied summaries derived from the separate notes volume.

entities.label: Joseph Hall, Selections from Early Middle English, 1130–1250, Part II: Notes (1920)
entities.description: Hall's companion notes volume, with manuscript descriptions, phonology, accidence, dialect, metre, introductions, line commentary, bibliographies, glossary, and index for the texts in Part I.

bibliographic.citation: Joseph Hall, Selections from Early Middle English, 1130–1250, Part II: Notes (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1920), pp. 223–675.
bibliographic.title: Selections from Early Middle English, 1130–1250. Part II: Notes

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Hall's 1920 Part II: Notes

notes: Direct title-page and page-text inspection identifies Part II and printed pp. 223–675; generic item metadata alone does not distinguish the part reliably.

entities.label: Worcester Cathedral Library MS F.174
entities.description: A Worcester manuscript containing grammatical material and the Worcester fragments; Hall prints selected material from ff. 63r–64r. Current scholarship dates the relevant manuscript context to the first half of the thirteenth century, later than Hall's circa-1180 estimate.

physical_description.extent: at least 66 folios represented in the scholarly description
notes: Relevant selected fragments at ff. 63r–64r; adjacent glossary and further fragments remain part of the physical object.

entities.label: British Library Royal MS 5 F VII
entities.description: A twelfth- to thirteenth-century parchment volume; Saint Godric's hymns occur on an inserted leaf at f. 85r.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Laud Misc. 636
entities.description: The Peterborough Chronicle manuscript; Hall's selection III uses the final continuation on ff. 88v–91v. Current cataloging dates the main copying to 1121–31 and the final continuation to the mid-twelfth century.

physical_description.support: parchment with inserted paper and binding matter
physical_description.extent: 91 medieval folios plus preliminaries, interleaves, and binding surfaces
notes: The complete 302-canvas Digital Bodleian object includes boards, inside boards, spine, edges, flyleaves, explicit blanks, and adjacent contents.

entities.label: British Library Harley Charter III B 49
entities.description: The charter cited by Hall for selection IV. Hall describes a French record hand using both insular and continental letter forms; an exact current item-record crosswalk was not verified.

holding.notes: Hall's designation retained pending modern catalog reconciliation.

entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Vespasian A XXII
entities.description: A composite manuscript; Hall prints A Parable from the Middle English portion, marked across ff. 56v–58r in the edition.

notes: The current catalog describes a composite volume; the Middle English section begins before Hall's selected folios.

entities.label: Jesus College, Oxford MS 29
entities.description: A trilingual chronicle and poetic anthology used by Hall for The Proverbs of Alfred, Memento Mori, and one Owl and Nightingale text. Hall's legacy foliation is current foliation +73 for all three portions.

physical_description.extent: 257 folios in the current complete digital object, with boards and flyleaves
notes: Current f. 189r–192r = Hall 262r–265r; current f. 179v–180v = Hall 252v–253v; current f. 156r = Hall 229r.

entities.label: Lambeth Palace Library MS 487
entities.description: A small West Midlands manuscript copied around the turn of the twelfth and thirteenth centuries. Hall uses Poema Morale on ff. 59v–65r and prose texts on ff. 15v–21v.

physical_description.extent: 68 folios (67 leaves in Hall's older description)

entities.label: British Library Egerton MS 613
entities.description: A manuscript containing two copies of Poema Morale. Hall uses the second copy on ff. 69r–70v and the first copy at f. 12v to complement the Lambeth text.

notes: The current complete IIIF object publishes 197 canvases from front through spine.

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge MS B.14.52
entities.description: A small thirteenth-century manuscript used by Hall for a Poema Morale copy on ff. 1r–8v and the Palm Sunday and Easter sermons on ff. 42v–46v.

physical_description.extent: 90 folios plus covers, flyleaves, stubs, and ten published UV alternates

entities.label: Corpus Christi College Cambridge MS 402
entities.description: A thirteenth-century Ancrene Wisse manuscript. Hall prints passages from ff. 56r–58r and 112v–117v and notes additions relative to the reconstructed original.

physical_description.extent: 118 folios plus preliminary and postliminary leaves and binding views

entities.label: Gonville and Caius College MS 234/120
entities.description: A late-thirteenth-century Ancrene Riwle manuscript. Hall prints selected Seven Deadly Sins material from ff. 116r–120v; his description notes 368 pages and extracts arranged differently from other copies.

entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Nero A XIV
entities.description: A second-quarter-thirteenth-century manuscript containing Ancrene Wisse on ff. 1r–120v and The Orison of Our Lady on ff. 120v–123v. Hall uses it as the base of one Ancrene Wisse selection and for selection XVIII.

entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Titus D XVIII
entities.description: A composite manuscript whose ff. 14–147 contain Ancrene Wisse and related works. Hall records unlettered Titus variants in selection IX and consultation for Sawles Warde.

physical_description.extent: 148 folios in Hall's description; relevant component ff. 14–147

entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Cleopatra C VI
entities.description: A composite manuscript containing an Ancrene Wisse component on ff. 4r–199r. Hall cites this witness as C, supplying passages between asterisks and variants for selection IX.

physical_description.extent: 203 folios in the composite current record; Ancrene Wisse ff. 4r–199r

entities.label: British Library Stowe MS 34
entities.description: An early-thirteenth-century manuscript written by three scribes and corrected by several hands. Hall selects Vices and Virtues passages from ff. 14v–16r and 47r–49r.

entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Caligula A IX
entities.description: A composite manuscript containing one Brut version and an Owl and Nightingale copy. Hall prints Laȝamon from ff. 81r–84v and uses the Owl witness alongside Jesus 29; Laȝamon is omitted and only the first Jesus page of Owl survives in CME00035.

physical_description.extent: 261 folios plus early modern endleaves and unfoliated flyleaves; 543 canvases in the current complete composite IIIF object
notes: Hall's older description calls the Brut manuscript 192 folios while discussing that component.
Hall Laȝamon selection: ff. 81r, column 2–84v, column 2.
The Owl and the Nightingale: ff. 233r–246r.

entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Otho C XIII
entities.description: A fire-damaged manuscript preserving a parallel Brut version used by Hall for selection XIV, on ff. 65v–67v. The selection is absent from CME00035.

notes: Damaged in the 1731 Cotton Library fire.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Junius 1
entities.description: The Ormulum manuscript, commonly treated as substantially authorial/autograph. Hall prints selection XV from ff. 34v–35v; the whole selection is omitted from CME00035.

physical_description.extent: 118 folios plus covers, flyleaves, stubs, and exterior views
notes: Current scholarship dates the copying approximately 1170–1185; Hall dated it about 1210.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Bodley 34
entities.description: An early-thirteenth-century devotional manuscript used for Sawles Warde through Hall line 339 and for a Saint Juliana parallel. Hall reports lost leaves after f. 80 and medieval associations with Ledbury, Godstow, and Much Cowarne.

physical_description.extent: at least 80 folios; leaves lost after f. 80

entities.label: British Library Royal MS 17 A XXVII
entities.description: An early-thirteenth-century manuscript containing Sawles Warde, Saint Katherine, and Saint Juliana. Hall uses ff. 10r–10v, 11r–14r, and 56r–61v respectively.

entities.label: British Library Arundel MS 292
entities.description: A late-thirteenth-century manuscript whose Middle English Bestiary begins at f. 4; Hall prints the work on ff. 4r–10v.

entities.label: Corpus Christi College Cambridge MS 444
entities.description: The unique manuscript of the Middle English Genesis and Exodus, dated about 1290–1310 in current aggregation. Hall selects Joseph and His Brethren from ff. 37v–49v.

physical_description.extent: 81 folios plus preliminary and postliminary leaves and binding views

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Laud Misc. 471
entities.description: A composite manuscript; Hall's Kentish Sermons come from the later component beginning at f. 92 and occupy ff. 128v, column 1–133v, column 1.

physical_description.extent: at least 133 folios in two bound manuscript components

entities.label: Complete Digital Bodleian object for MS Laud Misc. 636

notes: Upper board through upper-board fore-edge and tail; explicit boards and insides, spine, fore-edge, head, tail, flyleaf, angle views, and 57 blank surfaces. No canvas is explicitly labeled pastedown.

entities.label: Complete Digital Bodleian object for MS Junius 1

notes: Upper cover through tail; covers and insides, flyleaves, spine, fore-edge, head, tail, and four stubs. No canvas explicitly labels a blank or pastedown.

entities.label: Complete Digital Bodleian object for Jesus College MS 29

notes: Upper board through tail; boards and insides, four flyleaves, spine, fore-edge, head, tail, all ff. 1r–257v, and adjacent contents. The structure marks f. 155v blank; no canvas explicitly labels pastedowns.

entities.label: Complete published Trinity IIIF inventory for MS B.14.52

notes: Canvas 1 front cover; normal sequence ends at canvas 206 back cover; canvases 207–216 are UV alternates. Covers and insides, four flyleaves, manuscript leaves, and stubs are present; no spine, edge, head, tail, explicit pastedown, or explicit blank view is published.

entities.reproduction_format: IIIF JPEG page images and UV alternates

entities.label: Complete published Parker Library inventory for CCCC MS 402

notes: Front outside binding through back outside binding; outside and inside bindings, preliminary and postliminary leaves, and all ff. 1r–118v. No spine, edge, head, tail, or explicitly labeled pastedown/blank views.

entities.label: Complete published Parker Library inventory for CCCC MS 444

notes: Front outside binding through back outside binding; outside and inside bindings, preliminary and postliminary leaves, and all ff. 1r–81v. No spine, edge, head, tail, or explicitly labeled pastedown/blank views.

entities.label: British Library complete IIIF object for Egerton MS 613

notes: Published sequence begins with front cover and ends with spine.

entities.label: British Library complete IIIF object for Cotton MS Caligula A IX

notes: Published sequence begins with front cover and ends with spine; complete composite-object context is retained in the viewer.
